title: juncus fontanesii subsp. fontanesii
intro: Juncus fontanesii subsp. fontanesii, also known as Fontanes' rush or rush grass, is a type of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and potentially aiding in the management of blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Traditionally, the plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
